description: A high-cardinality label is attached to a metric, multiplying series until Prometheus runs out of memory.
body: Failure mode: a metric carries an unbounded label (user id, request id, full URL), so each unique value creates a new series and active series grow until the Prometheus server slows or OOMs for everyone. Detection signals: active series climbing without bound; server memory pressure; one metric dominating the series count. Do not label metrics with unbounded identifiers. Safer fallback: drop or aggregate the label via metric_relabel_configs, enumerate allowed values, and re-measure active series against the budget.
